key_points: Human activities are influenced by climate, soil, water, vegetation, and terrain
Settlements vary according to environmental conditions and resources available
Agriculture practices differ in mountains, plains, deserts, and coastal areas
Trade routes often develop along rivers, coasts, and passes through mountains
Disasters like floods, droughts, and cyclones affect economy, health, and settlements
examples: Terrace farming and irrigation in hills like Himachal Pradesh
Desert communities in Rajasthan using wells and camel transport
Delta regions like the Ganga-Brahmaputra support rice cultivation but face floods
Coastal towns trade via ports and fishing activities
Cyclones in Odisha and Tamil Nadu cause damage, prompting protective measures
formulas: Adaptation → Changing lifestyle according to environment
Modification → Altering environment for human benefit
Natural Hazard → Environmental event causing harm
Sustainability → Balancing resource use and conservation
exam_tips: Focus on examples showing human adaptation to different regions
Understand how environment shapes economic activities and settlements
Learn disaster-prone areas and mitigation measures
Be able to explain environmental modifications like irrigation and deforestation
